title: C and Python pubkey_to_iid use different derivation algorithms
description:
The C and Python implementations of pubkey-to-IID derivation differ:

- C (ipv6_addr.c): Uses first 8 bytes of Ed25519 pubkey directly
- Python (identity.py): Uses SHA-256(pubkey)[:8]

This means the same pubkey will produce different IIDs depending on which implementation is used, breaking interoperability.

Files:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:49-50
- python/src/lichen/crypto/identity.py:202-206

The Python approach (SHA-256 truncation) is safer because raw Ed25519 pubkey bytes have structure that could leak information. However, both implementations must agree.

Decision needed: which algorithm should be canonical, and update the other to match.
